sections.title: 2022 AMENDMENT
sections.text: The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty -fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.
sections.retrieval_text:
2022 AMENDMENT
level=1
The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty -fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.

page_spans.text: The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty -fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.

sections.title: LABELLING OF NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS
sections.text:
Information required in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
sections.retrieval_text:
LABELLING OF NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS
level=1
Information required in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

page_spans.text:
Information required in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text:
This standard applies to Anhydrous Milkfat, Milkfat, Anhydrous Butter oil, Butter oil and Ghee, which are intended for further processing or culinary use, in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

Anhydrous milkfat/ Anhydrous butter oil Milkfat Butter oil Ghee Maximum free fatty acids (% m/m as oleic acid) 0.3 0.4 0.4 0.4 Maximum peroxide value (milli-equivalents of oxygen/kg fat) 0.3 0.6 0.6 0.6 Taste and odour Acceptable for market requirements after heating a sample to 40–45°C Texture Smooth and fine granules to liquid, depending on temperature

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Food additives listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in Food Category 02.1.1 (Butter oil, anhydrous milkfat, ghee) may be used in foods subject to this standard.

number=4
level=1

page_spans.text: Food additives listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in Food Category 02.1.1 (Butter oil, anhydrous milkfat, ghee) may be used in foods subject to this standard.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The milk used in the manufacture of the products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the CAC.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sentences.original: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

classification.sentence: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.
classification.pattern: Introductory phrase indicating purpose + main clause prescribing required methods and reference standards with modal verb 'shall'

components.purpose: For checking the compliance with this standard
components.required_items: methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard

classification.justification: The sentence prescribes an obligation to use specified methods for compliance checking with normative modality 'shall'. The sentence structure includes an introductory purpose phrase followed by a prescriptive main clause. No known templates match closely, so a new pattern reflecting this structure is created.
notes: The sentence combines normative obligation with purpose indication in a complex sentence; no direct template found in catalog.

sentences.original: The name of the food shall be: Anhydrous milkfat Milkfat Anhydrous butter oil Butter oil Ghee According to description specified in Section 2, composition specified in 3 and the use of antioxidants (see Section 4).

classification.sentence: The name of the food shall be: Anhydrous milkfat Milkfat Anhydrous butter oil Butter oil Ghee According to description specified in Section 2, composition specified in 3 and the use of antioxidants (see Section 4).
classification.pattern: Mandative sentence prescribing mandatory naming with cross-references to description and composition sections

components.requirement: name of the food shall be

classification.justification: Clear normative obligation for food naming with specific options and cross-references; partial match to naming obligation templates, classified as adapted template.
notes: Mandative verb and multiple cross-references included

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR MILKFAT PRODUCTS
pages.summary: Página com título principal e cabeçalho repetitivo, sem outras seções detectadas.

Página contém dois títulos principais sem numeração: '2022 AMENDMENT' e 'LABELLING OF NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS', cada um com seu texto explicativo na página. Os títulos estão em caixa alta caracterizando nível 1. Texto está associado corretamente aos seus respectivos títulos. Não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas.

Página com hierarquia clara e numerada dos títulos principais e subtítulos, respeitando níveis de 1 a 2. Textos associados diretamente aos títulos e subtítulos correspondentes. Cabeçalho da página identificado. Não há continuidade de seções entre páginas nesta página.

A página apresenta dois títulos principais numerados (7 e 8) com seus respectivos subtítulos para o título 7. O texto está corretamente associado a cada nó, e não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas. O cabeçalho está presente na página.

A página contém três títulos numerados no nível 1: 'OTHER QUALITY FACTORS', 'OTHER CONTAMINANTS' e 'OTHER METHODS OF ANALYSIS'. Cada título apresenta seu respectivo texto detalhado. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado como 'CXS 280-1973  5'. Não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas.
